info.title: Food Delivery Service API
info.description: A RESTful API for a food delivery service with three roles (Customer, Restaurant Owner, Admin), order lifecycle management, coupon support, and comprehensive integration tests.

FieldsParam.name: fields
FieldsParam.description: Comma-separated field names to return (e.g. id,name,email)

post.description: Creates a new Customer or Restaurant Owner account and returns JWT tokens.
Authenticates a user and returns access + refresh JWT tokens.
Exchanges a valid refresh token for a new access + refresh token pair. Old refresh token is revoked (rotation).
Revokes all refresh tokens for the authenticated user.

patch.description: Update name and/or password for the authenticated user.

delete.description: Deletes a user. The built-in admin account cannot be deleted.

patch.description: Blocks or unblocks a user. Blocking revokes all refresh tokens and immediately invalidates the auth cache.

get.description: Customers see only unblocked restaurants. Owners see their own + unblocked. Admins see all.

patch.description: Owner can update own restaurant. Admin can update any.

delete.description: Cannot delete a restaurant with active orders (PLACED, PROCESSING, IN_ROUTE).

422.description: Cannot delete restaurant with active orders

patch.summary: Block or unblock a restaurant (Admin only)

get.description: Customers see only available, non-blocked meals. Owners/admins see all.

post.description: Owner can create meals for own restaurants. Admin can create for any.

delete.description: Cannot delete a meal that is part of an active order.

422.description: Cannot delete meal in active orders

post.description: Creates an order with one or more meals from a single restaurant. Optionally applies a coupon and adds a tip. Only customers can place orders.

422.description: Business logic error (expired coupon, usage limit, etc.)

get.description: Customers see own orders. Owners see orders for their restaurants. Admins see all.
Returns full order details including items, status history, and coupon info.

patch.description: Transitions the order to a new status following the state machine rules. Valid transitions: PLACED→PROCESSING (owner), PLACED→CANCELED (customer/owner), PROCESSING→IN_ROUTE (owner), PROCESSING→CANCELED (owner), IN_ROUTE→DELIVERED (owner), DELIVERED→RECEIVED (customer). Admin can perform any valid transition.
